title: Cephalomanes brassii
taxonomy: Cephalomanes brassii is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This classification places it within the division Pteridophyta, which comprises all vascular plants with leaves that are typically fern-like. The Hymenophyllaceae family is a group of filmy ferns, which are characterized by their thin, delicate leaves. Cephalomanes brassii is further classified within the genus Cephalomanes, which includes several species of ferns native to tropical regions. The classification of Cephalomanes brassii reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Cephalomanes brassii, also known as the Brassi's lip fern, is a species of fern belonging to the family Hymenophyllaceae. Traditionally, Cephalomanes brassii has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing pain and promoting overall well-being.
